data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. The express test expects `15.00` but the code returns `10.00`, so that one will fail.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with .NET 8. They want hot reload set up so they can iterate on failing tests. I can use `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests whenever files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can see the output and iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4adfda462a564773a88d23550c00117d && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
There's a compilation error - missing `using System;` for `ArgumentException`. Let me fix that, and then the watch will pick it up. Actually wait, the user said they want to iterate on failing tests. Let me fix the compile error so the tests can run, then the user can iterate on the actual test logic.

Actually, the user asked me to "set it up" - meaning set up hot reload. The watch is running. But there's a compile error. Let me fix the missing using directive so the tests can actually run.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running in the background. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00`, got `10.00`

The watcher will **automatically re-run all tests** every time you save a file. Just edit `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` (e.g., fix the `"express"` case to return `15.00m`) and the tests will re-execute within seconds.
